task.title: List every persisted session deterministically
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/486 by adding a read-only session discovery query over authoritative event histories without introducing a projection table, pagination, runtime/provider API, messages, or identity and tool policy.
acceptance_criteria: An empty store returns no sessions, while a populated store returns every session in ascending identifier order with its latest title, summary, lifecycle status, and active reason.
Listing is unchanged after reopening the database, excludes task streams with matching external identifiers, and appends no events.
Malformed payloads, invalid histories, and malformed owning session stream identifiers remain explicit errors rather than being skipped.
Concurrent writers cannot produce a mixed-time listing because discovery and full stream replay use one SQLite read snapshot.
Documentation, independent review, the local repository gate, required hosted checks, and squash merge complete successfully.

attempts.summary: Created bounded issue 486 and wrote session-listing acceptance tests before production support.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused RED suite failed to compile because SessionStore had no list method and SessionStoreError had no InvalidStreamId variant.
attempts.patch: Added integration scenarios for an empty store, deterministic ordering, latest projected state, database reopen, isolation from task streams, no-write behavior, malformed payloads, and malformed stream identities.

attempts.summary: Implemented event-based session discovery and reused session history projection.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused suite passed 34 tests after listing decoded creation events, derived validated session identifiers from owning streams, replayed full histories, and sorted projected sessions by opaque identifier bytes.
attempts.patch: Added SessionStore::list, SessionStoreError::InvalidStreamId, shared session projection logic, observable-contract documentation, and the README milestone entry.

attempts.summary: Fixed the independent review finding about inconsistent concurrent reads.
attempts.diagnostic: The first independent Codex review found that separate discovery and per-stream replay statements could mix snapshots under racing writers; the second review reported no actionable findings after the fix.
attempts.patch: Added a crate-private event-log query that selects and decodes every event from all matching streams in one ordered SQLite statement, then projects each session from that single read snapshot.

attempts.summary: Verified and published the bounded kernel capability.
attempts.diagnostic: Pinned just verify passed formatting, workspace checks, 113 tests, Clippy with warnings denied, SecretSpec integration, and diff checks; hosted Rust quality gate run 29461923200 passed. CodeRabbit's required status passed, while its comment explicitly reported a temporary review-volume limit rather than a substantive review.
attempts.patch: Committed the implementation as a Conventional Commit, linked issue 486 from pull request 487, enabled squash auto-merge, and monitored both required statuses.

attempts.summary: Squash-merged and synchronized the session discovery capability.
attempts.diagnostic: Pull request 487 merged as 38b8895970e6a0d491f2f111d68a62d8c1c6f00e after both required statuses passed, and local main fast-forwarded to the merge commit.
attempts.patch: Removed the remote feature branch through auto-merge and restored the local repository to synchronized main before recording the episode.

outcome.summary: Vela can now discover every persisted session in deterministic order from one consistent read snapshot while replaying authoritative histories and surfacing corrupt session data explicitly.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test session_lifecycle # RED: compile failure because list and InvalidStreamId did not exist
nix develop --command cargo test --locked -p vela-kernel --test session_lifecycle # GREEN: 34 passed, 0 failed
nix develop --command codex review --uncommitted # initial P2 mixed-snapshot finding fixed; repeat review had no findings
nix develop --command just verify # formatting, checking, 113 workspace tests, Clippy, SecretSpec, and diff gates passed
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29461923200 and required CodeRabbit status for pull request 487 # passed; CodeRabbit comment disclosed temporary review-volume limit

lessons: Session discovery can use the existing creation discriminator as a durable index seed without adding a second mutable projection.
A collection query over event-sourced aggregates needs one read snapshot; scanning identifiers and replaying streams in separate statements can return a mixed-time view under concurrent writers.
Selecting all events for matching streams in one ordered SQL statement preserves stream version validation and gives projection code a consistent input boundary.
Projection logic should be shared between load-by-identifier and collection queries so lifecycle ordering and corruption behavior cannot drift.
Stream namespaces isolate task and session aggregates even when their public identifiers are equal.
A required status can pass while an automated reviewer is rate-limited, so durable evidence must distinguish status completion from substantive review findings.
Read-only behavior and deterministic ordering are observable contracts worth direct tests rather than implementation assumptions.
